intro: You're going to wire up real Coil image loading: AsyncImage, its state machine, crossfades, and the memory cache. Every test uses coil-test's FakeImageLoaderEngine, so requests resolve instantly and offline, no real network needed. Each task has a failing test to turn green, and you can watch your work live in the Android Studio preview for each task.

tasks.title: AsyncImageStatus - load an image and track its state
tasks.detail: Load model with AsyncImage and track its AsyncImagePainter.State through the onState callback into a remembered status string, then render the image plus a 'Status: ...' line.
tasks.hint: var status by remember { mutableStateOf("Loading") }; Column(modifier) { AsyncImage(model, contentDescription, imageLoader, Modifier.size(64.dp), onState = { status = it.toStatusLabel() }); Text("Status: $status") }. toStatusLabel() already exists.
tasks.verify: T1AsyncImageStatusTest is green: after the fake request settles, the screen shows 'Status: Loaded' and the content description is present.

tasks.title: PlaceholderOrErrorImage - placeholder vs error state
tasks.detail: A null model shouldn't fire a request at all: show a 'No image' placeholder directly. A failed request (State.Error) needs a 'Couldn't load image' fallback instead of a blank space or a crash. Otherwise render normally.
tasks.hint: if (model == null) { Text("No image", modifier); return }; then the same status-tracking shape as AsyncImageStatus, but rendering just `status` (not prefixed with 'Status: ').
tasks.verify: T2PlaceholderOrErrorImageTest is green across all three cases: null model, a failing request, and a succeeding one.

tasks.title: crossfadeRequestFor - a request that fades in
tasks.detail: Build an ImageRequest that crossfades in on load instead of popping in abruptly. This is a plain function, not a composable; CrossfadeImage already calls it and is pre-written.
tasks.verify: T3CrossfadeRequestForTest is green: the built request's transitionFactory is a CrossfadeTransition.Factory with the given duration, and CrossfadeImage still renders the loaded image.

tasks.title: memoryCacheKeyFor - don't let a thumbnail and a full image collide
tasks.detail: Coil's default memory cache key doesn't account for the size an image was decoded at, so a thumbnail and a full-size load of the same URL can steal each other's cached bitmap. Build a MemoryCache.Key whose extras include the requested size.
tasks.hint: return MemoryCache.Key(key = url, extras = mapOf("w" to size.width.toString(), "h" to size.height.toString())).
tasks.verify: T4MemoryCacheKeyForTest is green: a 40x40 and a 400x400 key for the same url differ, but two 40x40 keys for the same url are equal.

tasks.title: SwappableImage - replacing a request when the model changes
tasks.detail: AsyncImage already cancels an in-flight request and starts a new one whenever model changes; the composable just needs to reflect the CURRENT model correctly, never a stale one.
tasks.hint: Same status-tracking shape as AsyncImageStatus, but the label includes the model: Text("$model: $status") and contentDescription = model?.toString().
tasks.verify: T5SwappableImageTest is green: swapping model between two different fake-intercepted urls always ends with the UI reflecting whichever model is current.
